inline_comments.message: yes. ves -> virtual network function event stream 😊

agreed, especially given this is like an ONAP PoC DMI it wil never be really big scale and any help for troubleshooting is good, we don't need to worry about excesive logging or performance etc.

if so can we name it more generically

consider:
VesEventsConfiguration.TopicNames
VesEventTopics.topics.names
as per per our convention use full classname

this is describe a condition wich is not being applied/tested here. 
just say when: 'the event is consumed'

dont talk about 'calling' methods etc. Just plain english
'the dmi service is registered correctly'
the correct exception is logged as a warning
the correct exception is logged as a warning

yeah.. i tried without this class but since there are multiple topics involved and want to use in the kafka listener.
I can as well hardcode both the topics here but then its not configurable.
hence prefer to have a class.

be consistent in your terms event/message decide/cm handle
short scenarios!
NB: shoudln't be mentioned here as it is of no significance for the test
Consume create event for existing cm-handle
why to 2diffrent code path, see my commet son the testware. I dont think this is needed!
cm handle(s) is registered with the dmi service

it really for any error:
Consume create event with error during registration
maybe remove this to avoid ambiguity
should the log contain thee original error ie. 'some error' if so use 'error for test' on line 78 and assert it is contained in the log
is there a different code path for generic exception then CmHandleRegistrationException if no, no test needed

These asserts are also true for the previous test! If there is really a difference we shoudl assert that! If not there should be no different code path/test

so based on this test/my comments I went back to the production code and my conclusion is theer should NOT be 2 code paths, you just handle 'any error during' registration
no. Since I am throwing CmHandleRegistrationException , hence the message from that exception is printed. Hence I am verifying that.

nope. same code path. hence removed the other test that checks for Exception.
Now i just check for CmHandleRegistrationException.

Is sourceName the right field to use? 
Would sourceID be better?
Or reportingEntityId or reportingEntityName ?
Is this for all VES events (not just PNFRegistration)? Register every time?
Suggest also test with more complicated CMHandles - e.g. where some characters need to be escaped, as would be common in a real device ID with mo-id/-path.
Suggest testing with a few other example PNF-registration events - in different formats - event if just the sourceName is used.

m listening from the below topics and i assume only the registration events are coming.

- "unauthenticated.VES_O1_NOTIFY_PNF_REGISTRATION_OUTPUT"
